Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 1: The "pyramid of merits" for peak-shaving and base-load gas turbines.**

    *   The diagram shows two pyramid-shaped diagrams
        *   Left: "Pyramid of Merits" for Peak-Shaving
        *   Right: "Pyramid of Merits" for Base-Load

    *   **Common Objective:** Gain per kWh, Max. Load Factor (located at the top of each pyramid)
    *   **Left Pyramid (Peak-Shaving):**
        *   Level 2:Control Costs of Maintenance, Fast Ramp-Up
        *   Level 3: Security of Fuel Supply, Startup Readiness
    *   **Right Pyramid (Base-Load):**
        *   Level 2:Control Fuel Cost, Plant Efficiency
        *   Level 3:Fuel Availability
    *   **Level 4 (base):** Safety & Emission Compliance (both pyramids share this aspect)
    *   **Level 5 (base):** The Prerequisites (both pyramids share this aspect)
    *   **Distinct Attributes:** connects the two pyramids.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Gas turbine "pyramid of merits" (described in Graphs/Charts)
    *   Components/Labels: Common Objective (Gain per kWh, Max Load Factor), Control Costs of Maintenance, Fast Ramp-Up, Security of Fuel Supply, Startup Readiness, Control Fuel Cost, Plant Efficiency, Fuel Availability, Safety & Emission Compliance, The Prerequisites, Distinct Attributes

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel cost represents up to 60% of O&M expenses for base-load or "semi-base-load" service.
*   Fuel Flex Involves Natural Gas (NG; common case) & Light Distillate (LD) in Gas Turbines

title: energies-16-03962-v3
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
**1. Mathematical equations**

WI = LHV/SG^0.5
MWI = LHV/(SG^0.5 x Tg^0.5)
ΔΤg = 2 × Tg × Δ(MWI)/MWI ≈ 2 × 300 × 0.06 = 36 Κ.

**2. Diagrams**

Components and labels:

**(a) Typical dual gas system:**
*   Backup Gas
*   SV4-1
*   GCV-1
*   TV6-1
*   Nozzle Orifices
*   Combust. chamber
*   SRV-1
*   Alternative Gas Orifices
*   Altern. Gas
*   SV4-11
*   CGV-11
*   SRV-11
*   Manifold 1
*   TV6-11
*   Nozzle Delta-P
*   Manifold 2
*   Nitrogen Purge
*   PV13-3
* Compressed Air Purge
* PV13-4

**(b) Double set of gas nozzles:**
*   Inner Cap
*   Conven'al gas orifices
*   Air swirlers
*   Outer (second'y) cap

*   SV4-1&11:Aux. Stop V.
*   SRV-1&11:Stop/ratio V.
*   GCV-1&11:Gas Ctrl V.
*   TV6-1&2:Transfer V.
*   PV13-3&4:Main Purge V

**3. Key numbers, constants, parameters**

*   ±5% MWI variation limit
*   Δ(MWI)/MWI = 0.06 (example)
*   Tg = 300 K (example)
*   ΔTg = 36 K (example)

**3. Tables:**
*   **Table 2. Similarities between biogenic and fossil fuels.**

| Biofuel                | Similar Fossil Fuel     | Commonnalities                                                                                                | Differences (Bio Versus Fossil)                                      |
| :--------------------- | :---------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :-------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Gases                  |                         |                                                                                                               |                                                                       |
| Biogas (raw)           | Weak Natural Gas (raw)  | - Comparable values of %C1 & WI                                                                             | - Higher CO2 & H2S contents - siloxane contaminants                |
| Liquids                |                         |                                                                                                               |                                                                       |
| DiMethyl-Ether (DME) | LPG                     | - High Vapor pressure - High vapor density                                                                   | - Narrower distillation range - burned as liquid (LPG as gas)         |
| Bioethanol-(biomethanol) | Naphtha - Gasoline    | - Intermiscible - Good purity - Highly volatile - Poor lubricity                                               | - Narrower distillation range - Lower NOx emission                  |
| Biodiesel              | Light Distillate Fuel (#2 DF) | - Intermiscible - Good to fair purity - limited volatility                                                      | - Better lubricity - Aggressive to gaskets                        |
| Vegetable Oil (VO) * | Crude oils              | - Wide distillation range - Partly intermiscible - Metallic impurities - Highly viscous                       | - No light hydrocarbon - No aromatic species - Less heat resistant - oil soluble contaminants |

**4. Diagrams:**
Diagram titled "H2 rainbow": the various types and production routes of dihydrogen.
*   H2 type: Black (Coal, Gasification + Shift), Grey (Natural Gas, Steam Reform. w/o CCUS), Blue (Natural Gas, Steam Reform. with CCUS), Turquoise (Natural Gas, HT Pyrolysis), Pink (Water, Electrolysis/Nuclear),
Yellow (Water, Electrolysis/Solar), Green (Water, Electrolysis/Wind & Hydro), White (H2 Reservoirs, Extraction)

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**
*   \%C1
*   WI
*   H2
*   O2
*   CO2
*   NOx
